headline: How The Cummins (CMI) Investment Story Is Shifting As Analysts Rework Targets And Assumptions
summary: Cummins is back in focus as fresh research work feeds into a higher modeled fair value of US$643.36 per share, up from US$631.40. That move sits alongside a cluster of price target changes that reflect mixed analyst views, with some raising targets by US$27 to US$123 and others trimming by about US$15, all based on different readings of growth and execution risk. Read on to see what these shifting targets may mean for you and how to follow the evolving narrative around the stock. Stay updated...

headline: Cummins Q1 Earnings Call Highlights
summary: Cummins (NYSE:CMI) reported first-quarter 2026 sales of $8.4 billion, up 3% from the year-ago period, as strong demand in power generation—particularly from data centers—more than offset weaker North America heavy- and medium-duty truck markets. headline: Cummins Refocuses Portfolio On Zero Emission Growth After Mixed Q1 Results
summary: Cummins (NYSE:CMI) reported mixed Q1 results and raised its 2026 outlook, citing demand tied to power systems and AI related applications. The company plans to divest its Low pressure Fuel Cell business while accelerating its zero emission technology strategy. These moves mark a meaningful shift in how Cummins is positioning its portfolio across traditional engines and newer clean power solutions. Cummins, a long established supplier of engines, power systems, and related components, sits...
